item.title: The OpenAI Bubble 摘要
item.description:
This article argues that the AI bubble is fundamentally an OpenAI bubble, with the company's survival underpinning trillions in capital expenditure. OpenAI plans to burn over $852 billion by 2030, accounting for the majority of hyperscaler RPOs and global AI compute spend. The author contends that without OpenAI's collapse—akin to Lehman Brothers—the industry remains in a state of cult-like psychosis, justifying massive capital misallocation. The piece traces OpenAI's role in reviving a struggling tech industry post-2022, with ChatGPT's success enabling hyperscalers to justify unprecedented GPU spending and infrastructure buildouts.
标签: AI, OpenAI, AI bubble, capital expenditure, tech industry, Sam Altman, NVIDIA, hyperscalers, ChatGPT, investment

item.title: Apple Intelligence OK’d to Launch in China, Using AI Models from Baidu and Alibaba 摘要
item.description:
Chinese regulators have granted Apple a license to launch Apple Intelligence in China, with Alibaba's Qwen model and Baidu's AI serving as technical partners. The Cyberspace Administration of China published the notice on Wednesday, also approving six other smartphone-based AI services from Samsung and Huawei. Alibaba confirmed its model will be integrated into iOS, iPadOS, macOS, and visionOS for Chinese users, enabling text and image generation. Baidu is also collaborating with Apple on AI features. The approval covers the initial Apple Intelligence announced two years ago, but it remains unclear if it extends to the recently announced Siri AI.
标签: Apple, Apple Intelligence, China, AI, Alibaba, Baidu, regulation, smartphone, LLM, technology

item.title: Apple Updates Advertising Services Policy With New Rules for Ads in Maps 摘要
item.description:
Apple published a new Advertising Services policy effective July 14, 2026, outlining rules for ads on Apple Maps. The policy prohibits home services businesses (plumbing, electrical, locksmith, HVAC, pest control, roofing, general contracting) and also bans deceptive, profane, political, weapon, violence, controlled substance, and defamatory ads. Apple's initial approach positions Maps ads as curated and navigation-focused rather than an extension of web search, though it may expand categories later. Critics argue the easiest way to avoid scammy ads is not to sell them in Maps at all.
标签: Apple, Apple Maps, advertising, policy, home services, scams, tech news, regulation

item.title: Eric Seufert: ‘Did Apple Just Signal a Third-Party Expansion of Apple Ads?’ 摘要
item.description:
Eric Seufert analyzes Apple's updated language in its advertising terms, which now includes 'other properties' beyond Apple-owned services. This broad phrasing suggests Apple may expand its advertising business to third-party surfaces, such as smart TVs and streaming devices. Seufert notes this expansion would explain why Apple did not update its AdAttributionKit (AAK) at WWDC 2026, as a third-party ad network would require a different attribution framework. The move could significantly increase Apple's advertising revenue and market reach.
标签: Apple, advertising, privacy, attribution, AdAttributionKit, WWDC, third-party, expansion, mobile, tech

item.title: Gurman on OpenAI’s Upcoming Hardware Product: ‘Movable, Screenless Speaker Built as AI Companion’ 摘要
item.description:
Bloomberg's Mark Gurman reports that OpenAI's upcoming hardware product is a movable, screenless speaker designed as an AI companion with a humanlike personality. It incorporates moving mechanical elements to feel alive, uses personal data like emails to understand users, and has a rechargeable battery for portability across rooms. The goal is to create a physical manifestation of ChatGPT. However, commentator John Gruber criticizes the design, arguing that if it can move, it should be autonomous like Star Wars droids, and that a device requiring manual carrying is not a true companion.
标签: OpenAI, hardware, AI companion, smart speaker, ChatGPT, robotics, product design, Bloomberg, Gurman, Gruber
